notes: Master-Reorg R-3b pill cleanup (part 1 — Rack Map). Redesign-only, legacy byte-identical. The re-homed pg-power subtab strip inside #rf-hw (Platforms/Hardware) carried a 'Rack Map' pill that duplicated the canonical Work RACK MAP card (rd_openOpsTool('rackmap')) — a one-door-per-feature violation. redesign_homeHardware now hides the Rack Map stab alongside the already-hidden Runbook stab (regex /pw-rb|pw-rm/), so under body.rd the Platforms strip shows Power / LED-GPU / Blast Radius / Thermal. NO stranding: Rack Map stays fully reachable via the Work RACK MAP card. Legacy untouched — redesign_homeHardware early-returns in FIELD mode, so ?legacy=1 shows pg-power with all six stabs, byte-identical. NOT in this ship (deferred, needs a design call — flagged to John): the Blast Radius pill move to the Deploy flow. Blast Radius is deployment-scoped (Design Law 2) but renderBlastRadius + 2 interactive sub-renders ALL hard-target the legacy #br-wrap id (3 call sites), so relocating it to Deploy is a real re-home of the node (or a parametrization of 3 render fns), not a pill hide — and hiding its pill without a new Deploy door would STRAND it (R-2b lesson). Its pill is intentionally retained until it has a Deploy home. No new assets (SW precache unchanged); CSS unchanged; 3-stamp lockstep. DEVICE-VERIFY (HARD-STOP, clear SW cache first): Crash Cart → PLATFORMS → open any platform → the sub-strip below has NO 'Rack Map' pill (Power/LED-GPU/Blast Radius/Thermal only); the Work RACK MAP card still opens Rack Map; ?legacy=1 still shows the full six-pill strip. ROLLBACK: revert the commit.
